HEXACO.rationale: The speaker is socially forward and lively (hosting, directing others, matchmaking) with some casually manipulative/controlling undertones and status/race talk (lower H), shows little fear or dependency (lower E_HEX), mixes light sarcasm with mild impatience (slightly low A_HEX), and offers few clear cues of strong conscientiousness or pronounced openness beyond some witty, reflective remarks.

The speaker comes across as socially forward and lively (many eager requests and conversational initiative), moderately curious and open to unusual ideas (interest in spiritualism and “extraordinary” phenomena), somewhat emotionally dependent/anxious (impatiently awaiting mother, seeking reassurance), with slightly low prudence/discipline (impulsive “do let us try at once”), and only mild evidence

The speaker shows some coercive/exploitative tendencies (e.g., demanding money and threatening others), relatively low expressed fear or dependence, mild social assertiveness in questioning/leading others, some irritability/hostility in warnings, and otherwise fairly neutral signs of organization or intellectual/aesthetic openness in these practical, situation-driven utterances.

The speaker stresses inner sincerity over outward show and appeals to honesty and fellowship (moderate Honesty-Humility), yet is frequently sarcastic and cutting (“tedious old fools,” sexual innuendo) and driven by revenge (low Agreeableness); they show some anxiety/bad dreams and brooding suspicion (moderately high Emotionality) while being neither strongly outgoing nor withdrawn, and they displa

The speaker shows strong anxiety/sentimentality and dependence on others for confidence (high Emotionality), some touchiness and self-reproach but little clear hostility (near-neutral Agreeableness), modest deference without evidence of manipulation (slightly positive Honesty-Humility), only mild social boldness amid hesitation (slightly positive Extraversion), some impulsive/excitable disorganiza

The speaker comes off as socially bold and lively (frequent teasing, banter, directing others), but notably sneering and mocking with sharp, hostile jabs; there’s little evidence of anxiety or dependence, only mild curiosity and average prudence, while the tone suggests low humility/fairness via condescension and manipulative needling.

The speaker is highly sentimental and love-preoccupied (high Emotionality) with rich poetic, aesthetic language about music and passion (very high Openness), shows some social initiative but also prefers being least in company (slightly low-to-mid Extraversion), presses others to act clamorous and overstep bounds and makes sweeping disparaging claims about women (slightly low Agreeableness), shows

The speaker is domineering and status-assertive (“stronger and more of a man”), expresses hostile, prejudiced, and controlling attitudes (racial-supremacist claims; “oughtn’t to let her run around”), shows social boldness and high talkativeness, little visible anxiety, some everyday prudence/competence (“bond man,” organized social introductions), and relatively closed, dogmatic ‘scientific’ certa

The speaker presents as socially fluent and welcoming (many polite greetings, invitations, lively talk) with some principled/justice-oriented remarks and a measured stance against jealousy, but shows moderate self-focus and status/agenda management (committee, council act, proving devotion) rather than clear humility; emotionality is fairly controlled, conscientiousness somewhat purposeful yet not

The speaker is romantically intense and vulnerable (high Emotionality) with bold, approach-oriented courtship and social ease in dialogue (moderately high Extraversion), generally gentle rather than hostile (slightly high Agreeableness) and not overtly exploitative though somewhat self-dramatizing (slightly high Honesty-Humility), but shows impulsive, imprudent risk-taking and poor self-regulation

The speaker is polite and somewhat principled but also engages in gossip-driven inference (letter glimpse, asking for concealment), shows strong anxiety and distress with dependent appeals to another’s prudence, is socially restrained rather than bold, generally gentle and apologetic, fairly prudent/organized in actions described, and only mildly signals aesthetic/intellectual curiosity (brief tas

The speaker shows principled protectiveness and willingness to keep secrets rather than exploit others (moderately high Honesty-Humility), strong fear/anxiety and religious-sentimental concern about sin and ruin (high Emotionality), a largely defensive/withdrawn interpersonal style with refusals to speak (slightly low Extraversion), some sharp accusatory language but also appeals to mercy and righ

The speaker presents as principled and sincere in love (seeking honorable marriage and warning against false oaths), highly sentimental and anxious about danger and betrayal, moderately socially bold but often private/guarded, generally gentle and forgiving, somewhat prudent/planful in arranging messages and timing though also impulsive in passion, and markedly imaginative and philosophically inqu

The speaker is sociable and party-oriented (high X) with some prudence and emphasis on carefulness (moderately high C), shows mild skepticism/gossiping and a bit of sharp judgment (“I hate careless people,” “tantalizing you”) suggesting slightly lower A and only slightly positive H, while displaying little anxiety or dependence (slightly low E) and some witty, unconventional observations about par

The speaker shows high emotionality and dependence (worrying about fault, pleading with “mamma,” religious crying, yearning/romantic sensitivity) alongside moderate sociability (many social questions, greetings, ball talk) and mild gentleness/forgivingness (“If you can forgive me”), with little direct evidence of strong principled fairness or exploitation, and limited cues for conscientious self-d

The speaker is forceful and punitive ("You will get six months. Now march!"), shows low fear/anxiety and high toughness in crisis, focuses on procedure/authority and quick commands (moderate conscientiousness), exhibits little warmth/forgiveness, and offers little evidence of modesty or curiosity beyond practical problem-solving.

The speaker is thoughtful and principled but willing to deceive via disguise for safety (slightly low-to-neutral H), openly anxious and conflicted about love and fate (high E_HEX), socially capable yet not especially gregarious (slightly high X), generally polite with flashes of sharpness and cynicism rather than steady gentleness (near-neutral A_HEX), plans carefully and works diligently at roles

Across the dialogue the speaker comes off as self-reliant and not very anxious, somewhat reserved rather than socially bold, moderately conscientious and reflective in how she answers, mildly open-minded/analytical, with only slight signs of humility and generally even-tempered but willing to voice firm dislikes (e.g., about Brocklehurst).

The speaker presents as sincerely principled and service-oriented (offering help without seeking credit), emotionally tender and worried, notably forgiving and gentle in religiously framed consolation, only mildly socially bold, reasonably dutiful in taking on practical responsibilities despite admitting weakness, and not especially characterized by unconventional or highly exploratory thinking.

The speaker is polite and duty-focused but also status-conscious and somewhat politically instrumental (recruiting others to monitor Hamlet), shows clear fear and distress under threat (“Help, help, ho!”; shocked reactions), is moderately socially engaged in formal courtly contexts, mixes gentle admonitions with moments of sharp rebuke, appears fairly prudent/managerial, and shows little evidence

The speaker shows strong anxiety, hurt, and dependence on reassurance (“everything’s lost,” “think for me…help me,” “I am afraid”), marked unforgiving anger and vengefulness (“I have nothing but hatred… I could kill him”) with little evidence of sociable assertiveness, curiosity, or disciplined problem-solving; honesty-humility cues are mixed, with some fairness (“Anna is in no wise to blame”) but

The speaker is principled about honor and not misleading others (rejecting Orsino, apologizing for the ring), but is sharp-tongued and impatient with “fools” and “madman” talk; they show some guarded vulnerability about reputation and being judged, moderate social assertiveness in directing servants and managing visitors, and a generally orderly, prudent, rhetorically sophisticated manner.

The speaker is socially engaged and inquisitive at a party (moderate Extraversion/Openness), shows some impatience and blunt criticism ('Don’t talk…', 'You’re a rotten driver') suggesting lower Agreeableness, while utterances provide little clear evidence of manipulation, strong anxiety, or strong conscientious planning.

The speaker presents a polished, duty-and-order-focused public persona with confident courtly command and careful statecraft (high C, moderately high X), but shows manipulative/expedient tendencies (using others as “lawful espials,” instrumental flattery, political maneuvering) and a somewhat harsh, moralizing impatience toward grief (lower H and A), while expressing relatively little anxiety or d

The speaker is overtly willing to deceive and orchestrate murder for power (“look like the innocent flower, but be the serpent under’t”), shows callousness and coercive hostility toward others (low Honesty-Humility and Agreeableness), remains relatively bold and emotionally controlled under threat with only brief fear/sentiment (“my father as he slept”), and demonstrates strong planning/decisivene

The speaker shows strong anxiety and emotional agitation with guilt-themed rumination (high Emotionality), some irritability/blame and impatience toward others (slightly low Agreeableness), modest organization/prudence mixed with confusion (slightly above-neutral Conscientiousness), little clear social boldness (near-neutral Extraversion), mild imaginative/reflective description (slightly above-ne

The speaker is intense and sentimental (frequent regret, alarm, and strong feeling), highly aesthetic and intellectually picky about “taste,” books, and language, but also judgmental and easily affronted (threatening to leave the room, harsh critiques), with only modest evidence of social boldness or disciplined/prudent self-control beyond emotional frankness.

The speaker is highly sociable and socially bold (easy invitations, sustained chat), generally polite and conciliatory (apologies, reassurance), shows some sentimentality and concern about others’ impressions, but also self-aggrandizes and manages impressions with dubious-sounding life claims while showing only mild evidence of diligence/organization and moderate curiosity/aesthetic appreciation.

The speaker is generally sincere and grateful but not especially self-effacing, shows high sentimentality/anxiety (frequent tears, fate, distress over loss), is only mildly socially forward, oscillates between warmth and sharp anger/threats in conflict, displays some prudence and resolve without strong organization cues, and shows notable imaginative/poetic reflection and curiosity about “memorial

The speaker often endorses or role-plays exploitative violence ("kill the people and take their watches and money"), shows some anxiety/sentimentality and attachment (panic about deaths, affectionate relief at reunion), is moderately talkative but not consistently socially bold, shows flashes of irritability and stubborn skepticism, mixes some prudence/planning with impulsive criminal fantasies, a

The speaker shows anxious, nerve-focused emotional reactivity and dependence on social outcomes (especially marriage prospects), is talkative and socially engaged but also irritable and dismissive (“Nonsense,” snapping at Kitty), with little evidence of principled modesty or intellectual/aesthetic curiosity beyond conventional admiration of status and dresses.

The speaker repeatedly expresses fear, distress, and emotional dependence on family (“What shall I do!”, “I fear…”, “I implore you…”, “think of me. As a wife and mother!”), shows sincerity and selfless concern for loved ones rather than exploitation, is generally gentle and pleading rather than hostile, is not especially socially bold, and gives little evidence of strong conscientious organization

The speaker is talkative, socially forward, and lively (high X), shows some warmth and sentiment toward the child she nursed but also snaps/teases others (“Out upon you!”) (slightly low A_HEX), is not especially fearful or dependent (near-neutral E_HEX), displays only mild principled sincerity alongside a small money-minded remark (“shall have the chinks”) (slightly positive H), and gives little e

The speaker shows pride and status-sensitivity alongside some principled loyalty to his father (near-neutral Honesty-Humility), relatively low fearfulness and strong self-reliance in confrontational patriotic outbursts (low Emotionality), moderate social boldness and talkativeness (mid Extraversion), a quickness to contempt/hostility and harsh political insults (low Agreeableness), mixed signals o

The speaker is highly sentimental, anxious, and emotionally dependent (intense focus on and longing for Cosette, fear about illness and separation), shows little clear evidence of manipulation or principled fairness either way, has only mild signs of sociability, flashes some impatience/irritation (“Answer me!”, “Was I speaking to you?”) but not sustained hostility, appears somewhat scattered rath

Across the utterances the speaker is deferential and principled (returns gifts, obeys commands, values honesty), highly fearful and emotionally shaken, socially restrained and dependent on authority figures, generally gentle and non-hostile, fairly dutiful and self-controlled, and only mildly inquisitive/creative aside from vivid, poetic description.

The speaker is sharp-tongued, contemptuous, and quick to moralize and threaten reporting others (low Agreeableness), yet dutifully delivers messages and enforces household order with prudence (higher Conscientiousness); he shows little anxiety or dependence (low Emotionality), some self-important fantasizing and status-seeking (lower Honesty-Humility), moderate social presence without clear warmth

The speaker is candid and self-critical about moral errors and limits (“honestly give,” admits “very wrong”), generally gentle and principled rather than exploitative, somewhat reserved and low in social boldness, emotionally moderate with some sentimentality about the past, relatively prudent and conscientious in moral reasoning, and only mildly open/intellectual—preferring utility and convention

Across the excerpts the speaker presents as principled and fair-minded (defends others, discourages boasting), emotionally warm but not especially anxious, mildly reserved yet capable of steady social engagement, generally patient and gently teasing rather than hostile, prudent and orderly in judgment, and notably interested in literature/taste and nuanced aesthetic discussion.

The speaker presents as dutiful and generally fair-minded with no signs of manipulation (moderate H), strongly fear- and awe-reactive to ominous events (high E_HEX), more reserved than socially bold (slightly low X), gentle and deferential rather than hostile (moderately high A_HEX), reasonably prudent/observant but not especially planful or self-directed (slightly positive C_HEX), and notably ref

The speaker is generally fair and non-exploitative (offers to pay and part “without ill-blood”) but also self-indulgent and cynical (“except wine like this”), shows mild vulnerability/confusion without strong fearfulness, is moderately socially forward in conversation yet not especially lively, is mostly non-hostile though occasionally sharp (“why the devil don’t you dine?”), hints at low prudence

The speaker shows opportunism and willingness to profit (“make some money”), strong hostility and aggression (“Throw me down and beat me”), some anxious/complaining vulnerability (“I’m sick…all run down”), moderate social assertiveness (calling out “Daisy!” repeatedly), and little evidence of careful planning or intellectual/aesthetic curiosity beyond basic consumer interest in a dog.

The speaker is status- and patronage-fixated and self-serving in courtship (pressuring, negating refusals, invoking rank/connection), relatively emotionally steady and self-reliant, moderately socially forward and talkative, irritable/ungentle in dismissing others’ agency and worth, quite prudent/planful and duty-focused, and only mildly intellectually curious beyond conventional moralizing.

The speaker shows notable anxiety and alarm ("I am mad! I am dreaming!", worries about eyesight) alongside some practical, directive behavior (giving instructions, offering payment for shelter), with little evidence of warmth/forgiveness or strong curiosity/creativity, and only modest indications of honesty-humility beyond fair transactional intent.

The speaker is highly prudent, rule-setting, and strategic (detailed precepts, careful monitoring, and organized indirect inquiry), but shows manipulative/deceptive tendencies ("bait of falsehood" and instructing forgeries), a controlling and suspicious interpersonal stance with some irritability, while displaying only mild anxiety/concern and neither notably outgoing nor especially imaginative.

The speaker shows some practical diligence and prudence (detailed talk about cutting grass, giving money for a loaf) but also some irritability/harshness and contempt (“Get along with you!”, “How ugly she is…”, “malicious creature”), with little evidence of anxiety, sociable confidence, or intellectual/aesthetic openness beyond concrete, workmanlike concerns.

The speaker is generally sincere and socially considerate (offers introductions, clarifies intentions), shows low anxiousness and emotional dependence, comes across as moderately sociable and witty but also somewhat teasing/critical at times, displays decent prudence and self-reflection in argument, and shows notable curiosity about character and nuanced discussion, suggesting higher openness.

The speaker presents as socially fluent and companionship-seeking, but also bitter and accusatory (low forgiveness/gentleness), with hints of self-serving grievance and possible manipulation in framing events, modest anxiety/sentiment tied to past attachments, and only mixed signals of prudence/discipline and intellectual openness.
